title: Run Arch-only shell tests in CI and harden worktree stubbing
objective: The Arch-dependent shell tests (pacman wrapper, makepkg wrapper, hosts-guard hooks) have never run anywhere, so they rot unnoticed. Give them an archlinux:base-devel container job, repair the ones still pointing at pre-reorganisation paths, and remove the failure mode where test_music_parallelism.sh could silently source the real installed common.sh instead of its stub — which previously made a healthy daemon look broken. Success means CI runs every shell test that can meaningfully run, and any future path drift fails loudly with an explanatory message instead of masquerading as a behavioural bug.
acceptance_criteria: A new arch-tests job runs test_hosts_file_monitor.sh, test_makepkg_capped.sh, test_makepkg_wrapper.sh, test_pacman_wrapper_security.sh and test_shutdown_timer_monitor.sh in archlinux:base-devel, and all five pass.
Those five pass with --network=none locally, proving the job needs no package installs beyond git.
test_music_parallelism.sh derives its temp-worktree path from the script's real location, so a future reorganisation cannot silently change the depth.
test_music_parallelism.sh fails with an explanatory message, not a wrong number, if the stub is unreachable: a pre-flight resolution check plus a marker the stub writes and every run asserts.
The existing ubuntu shell-tests job (6 tests + 30 bats assertions) still passes unchanged.
Every excluded test carries a comment in the workflow naming the specific reason it is excluded.
out_of_scope: test_i3blocks_efficiency.sh — needs strace and SYS_PTRACE; could not be validated locally because containers on this machine have no DNS, so it is excluded rather than shipped unverified.
test_hosts_guard_pacman_integration.sh — after the path repair it fails a genuine assertion about pacman-hook detection; that is a real finding to investigate, not CI plumbing.
test_security_hardening.sh — asserts live host state such as an immutable /etc/hosts, so it can never pass in a fresh container.
Any change to the scripts under test. Every failure addressed here was in the tests or the workflow.
Fixing this machine's container DNS, which is what blocked validating the strace-dependent test.
verifier: docker run --rm --network=none -v $PWD:/repo:ro -w /repo archlinux:base-devel bash -c '<five tests>' for the container set; bats linux_configuration/tests/*.bats plus the six ubuntu-set tests on the host; pre-commit run --all-files and the pre-push ci-mirror gate; finally the Shell tests workflow itself on GitHub Actions.
